content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Mr. Gareth J. Williams                    Date : June 25, 1976
    Executive Secretary
    Board of Osteopathic Examiners
    921 11th Street, Suite 1201
    Sacramento, California 95814

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION - Edgar A. Kerry, Staff Attorney
       Conflicts of Interest Division

Subject: Conflict of Interest Code for the Board of Osteopathic Examiners


       Thank you for your memo of June 22, 1976, requesting an
extension of the July 10, 1976, deadline for submission of your
Conflict of Interest Code to the Fair Political Practices
Commission.

       As indicated to you by telephone, at its July 6, 7, 8, 1976,
meeting, the Commission will consider adoption of a regulatory
procedure to permit an agency with no "designated employees" to
request exemption from the requirements of adopting a Conflict of
Interest Code. I enclose a copy of the draft regulation (2 Cal. Adm.
Code Section 18751) for your review.

       To permit your agency to take advantage of this new proposed
regulation, the deadline for submission of your Conflict of
Interest Code is extended from July 10, 1976, to August 10, 1976.

       I call your attention to Section 18751(c) of the proposed
regulation. To insure that your agency is prepared to submit its
request for exemption immediately upon approval of the proposed
regulation, you should take action to meet the noticed hearing
requirements as soon as possible.


EK:mra
Attachment

State of California

Memorandum

To      :   Mr. Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr.
            Chief, Conflict of Interest Division
            Fair Political Practices Commission
            1100 K Street Building
            Sacramento

Date: May 19, 1976

From : Board of Osteopathic Examiners (916) 322-4306

Subject: Conflict of Interest Code


In response to your memorandums of April 26, 1976 and April 30, 1976,
the Board of Osteopathic Examiners requests an informal opinion from
your office regarding the necessity of our preparing a conflict of
interest code.

Upon careful review of your guidelines, the Board and its legal
counsel are of the opinion that it has "No participation in the making
of governmental decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect
on any financial interest". The Board functions only ministerially.
It licenses osteopathic physicians and surgeons and makes no decisions
that have a material fiscal effect.

In light of the above, and in consideration of Section D, California
Administrative Code 18700, it is our conclusion that this Board is
exempt from such requirement, and that to mandate this board to prepare
and submit a conflict of interest code would be counterproductive.


[signature]
GARETH J. WILLIAMS
Executive Secretary

GJW/bm
